channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Pope remains stable after month in hospital as he continues treatments and works from bedside
item.description: Pope Francis continues to improve a month after he was hospitalized with bronchitis and double pneumonia, the Vatican’s Holy See Press Office said in an update on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: VOA journalists put on administrative leave after Trump axes parent agency
item.description: Journalists at Voice of America (VOA) were put on administrative leave on Saturday, a day after President Trump signed an executive order aimed at eliminating the U.S. Agency for Global Media (USAGM), VOA’s parent company.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Russia and Ukraine trade overnight aerial attacks after Putin sets out conditions for ceasefire
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia and Ukraine traded heavy aerial blows overnight, with both sides reporting Saturday more than 100 enemy drones over their respective territories. The attacks comes less than 24 hours after Russian President Vladimir Putin met with U.S.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says ISIS chief killed in Iraq
item.description: President Trump on Friday announced that ISIS’s second in command was killed in Iraq this week, touting it as an example of his administration’s “peace through strength” military philosophy.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, The Hill

item.title: Why Trump is singling out South Africa and accusing it of being anti-white and anti-American
item.description: CAPE TOWN, South Africa (AP) — The Trump administration’s decision to expel the South African ambassador is its latest move against a country it has singled out for sanctions and accused of being anti-white and anti-American. U.S.
item.dc:creator: GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Pope works on signature reform from hospital as Vatican cuts back on updates on medical progress
item.description: Pope Francis has entered the fifth week of hospital treatment for double pneumonia very much looking ahead as he worked on a signature priority of his papacy.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Barry, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Starmer challenges Putin to prove he is serious about peace by signing up to Ukraine ceasefire
item.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer has challenged Russian President Vladimir Putin to sign up to a ceasefire in its war against Ukraine now, if he is serious about peace.
item.dc:creator: Pan Pylas, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Rubio declares South African ambassador to US 'persona non grata' over Trump comments
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio declared South African ambassador to the U.S. Ebrahim Rasool “persona non grata” and accused the diplomat of being a “race-baiting politician” over his recent comments about President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Thousands converging on Serbia's capital for a major rally that tests Vucic's populist government
item.description: BELGRADE, Serbia (AP) — Tens of thousands of people swarmed the streets in Serbia's capital Belgrade on Friday, staging a joyful prelude to a major anti-government rally on the weekend and dealing a blow to populist President Aleksandar Vucic.
item.dc:creator: JOVANA GEC, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Mark Carney is sworn in as Canada's new prime minister as country deals with Trump's trade war
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Former central banker Mark Carney was sworn in as Canada’s new prime minister on Friday, and will now try to steer his country through a trade war brought by U.S. President Donald Trump, annexation threats and an expected federal election.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Greenland political parties join together against Trump comments about annexing the island
item.description: LONDON (AP) — All five parties in Greenland’s parliament issued a joint statement on Friday rejecting President Donald Trump’s latest effort to take control of the strategic Arctic island.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas says it will release a US-Israeli hostage and 4 bodies, but Israel expresses immediate doubt
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Palestinian militant group Hamas said Friday that it has accepted a proposal from mediators to release one living American-Israeli hostage and the bodies of four dual nationals who had died in captivity.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: House GOP promotes use of 'Taiwan' over 'Chinese Taipei' in slight to Beijing
item.description: A group of House Republicans is introducing legislation to assert Taiwan as the official name of the independently-governed island in U.S. communication, pushing back against claims to the territory by the Chinese government in Beijing. Rep. Byron Donalds (R-Fla.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Mark Carney is sworn in as Canada's new prime minister
item.description: Carney, a former Goldman Sachs executive with no experience in politics, becomes Canada's 24th prime minister.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says he's asked Putin to spare Ukrainian troops' lives
item.description: President Trump said he strongly requested that Russian President Vladimir Putin spare the lives of Ukrainian soldiers surrounded by Russian troops, in a statement posted to his social media site. Trump described “good and productive” discussions between U.S.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: American draws Australian PM's ire after snatching wombat
item.description: Australian immigration officials could bar an American influencer from ever returning Down Under for carrying a baby wombat away from its mother.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: UN World Food Program to cut food aid for over 1 million people in Myanmar
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — The United Nations food agency said on Friday that more than 1 million people in the war-torn nation of Myanmar will be cut off from food assistance due to critical funding shortfalls.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas agrees to release Edan Alexander, the last living American hostage
item.description: Hamas agreed Friday to release Edan Alexander, the last living American hostage in captivity in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Europe's military personnel shortfalls exposed as Trump warns US security priorities lie elsewhere
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — In the year after Russia launched outright war on Ukraine, NATO leaders approved a set of military plans designed to repel an invasion of Europe. It was the biggest shake-up of the alliance’s defense readiness preparations since the Cold War.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Netanyahu slams 'terrorist-supporting' UN council that accused Israel of committing sexual crimes
item.description: Israeli and American officials are condemning a report from the United Nations Human Rights Council that accuses Israel of carrying out systemic acts of sexual violence against Palestinians.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Putin says he agrees in principle with US proposal for 30-day ceasefire in Ukraine
item.description: “So the idea itself is correct, and we certainly support it,” Putin told a news conference in Moscow.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump meets with NATO chief Mark Rutte
item.description: The meeting comes after Trump called on European allies to contribute more to the alliance's defense fund.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Pakistan says insurgents who attacked a passenger train killed 21 hostages while all others freed
item.description: QUETTA, Pakistan (AP) — Insurgents who attacked a passenger train carrying 440 passengers in restive southwestern Pakistan killed 21 hostages before security forces killed all 33 of the assailants, and all other passengers were rescued, officials said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ABDUL SATTAR and MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Russia hits Zelenskyy's hometown as Ukraine signals it's ready for peace
item.description: As Ukraine appeared to pivot towards peace, Russia launched a deadly attack on Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy's hometown of Kryvvi Rih.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Greenland's center-right party pulls off upset victory as Trump seeks control
item.description: Greenland's center-right Demokraatit party saw a sharp increase in support as it won the country's election on Tuesday. Independence became a central issue as President Donald Trump talked of a U.S. takeover.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukraine, US say country open to 30-day ceasefire in war with Russia
item.description: Ukraine says in a joint statement with the United States that it is open to a 30-day ceasefire in its war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pro-Palestinian activists vandalize Trump golf course in Scotland
item.description: In white bold-face capital letters, activists wrote “Gaza is not 4 sale” on Trump’s Turnberry golf course.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Ontario will tariff electricity going to 3 US states on Monday, premier says
item.description: The leader said new tariffs are scheduled to be implemented Monday in an effort to push back on Trump’s international economic measures.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation
